_doc: Service -> payout-sender lookup. Used by scripts/realized_revenue_collector.py to classify incoming on-chain transfers to your main wallet. Match is case-insensitive on the `from` field of incoming token/native transfers. Multiple addresses per service supported (some services rotate hot wallets).
_classification_order: 5. Fall through to `unknown` and log to tmp/realized_revenue_unknown_senders.json for triage
_filling_this_in: When you receive your first payout from a service, copy the `from` address from the explorer and paste it here under the matching service. The collector will pick it up on the next run.
Until then, any payout will land under `unknown` — that's fine, it's reversible.

pawns._note: Pawns/IPRoyal pays USDT-ERC20 from a hot wallet. First payout will reveal the actual sender — paste here.

earnfm._note: EarnFM pays via internal wallet system; on-chain payouts only after $15 threshold. Likely Solana USDC.

bytelixir._note: ByteLixir pays USDT-TRC20 (TRON). Out of scope for the Etherscan-family Layer 1 collector. Add TronScan support if/when ByteLixir hits payout threshold.

anyone_protocol._note: Anyone Protocol pays ANYONE token. Addresses populate on first payout.

exclusions._doc: Things that look like service revenue but aren't. Each match here causes the row to be silently dropped from the realized-revenue feed.

personal_inbound._note: Example: CEX withdrawal address you used to fund the wallet. NEVER counted as revenue.

dex_routers._note: Example: Uniswap V3 universal router. Self-swaps come back via routers; not service revenue.

_token_service_heuristic._doc: Token symbol -> service mapping. Used as a last-resort classifier when sender-address match fails.

_token_contracts._doc: Per-chain reverse map of token contract addresses. Populate as needed; the collector only uses this to translate Etherscan tokenSymbol fields when they're missing.
